components.desc: Module plugin_loader.py (stdlib only). Reads site.json "plugins": [...] list, imports each name from plugins/ via importlib, dispatches hooks in list order: on_page(page)->dict pre-render, on_html(page, html)->str post-nav-injection, on_site(site, out_dir)->None post-build. All hooks optional (missing attrs skipped silently). Unknown plugin name: clear error to stderr, nonzero exit. Any hook raising: build fails, plugin name in stderr, nonzero exit. Exposes load_plugins(config) plus run_on_page/run_on_html/run_on_site helpers callable from forge.py. check.sh: tmp dir fixture with trivial plugin recording hook call order to a file, asserts all three hooks fire in order; unknown-name run exits nonzero with name in stderr; raising plugin exits nonzero with name in stderr; trap cleanup, <60s.

plugins/README.md documenting the hook contract: exact signatures on_page(page: dict)->dict, on_html(page: dict, html: str)->str, on_site(site: dict, out_dir: str)->None; page dict shape {path,name,title,md,html}; site dict {pages,config}; execution order (list order from site.json), all-hooks-optional rule, error semantics (raise = build fails loudly, name in stderr). check.sh: greps README.md for each hook signature string, page/site dict key names, and error-semantics keywords; nonzero if any missing.

plugins/highlight.py exposing on_html. Finds fenced code blocks in rendered HTML with a language class; python blocks get <span class="tok-kw">, tok-str, tok-com, tok-num wrapping keywords, strings, comments, numbers; unknown languages get generic fallback (tok-str + tok-num only); blocks without lang tag pass through byte-identical. Operates on already-HTML-escaped content, must not double-escape; regex/tokenize stdlib only. check.sh: tmp fixture HTML with python block, unknown-lang block, untagged block; run on_html directly; assert tok-kw/tok-str/tok-com/tok-num present for python, tok-str/tok-num for unknown, untagged block bytes unchanged, and output parses cleanly via html.parser with no nested/unclosed spans.

plugins/rss.py exposing on_site. Writes RSS 2.0 feed.xml at out_dir root: channel title/link/description from site.json config keys; one <item> per page with title, link, description = first paragraph text extracted from page html. Deterministic: fixed or omitted pubDate, never wall clock. stdlib only (xml.etree or string building with proper escaping). check.sh: tmp fixture site dict with 2-3 pages; call on_site twice into two dirs; parse feed.xml with xml.etree.ElementTree, assert channel meta present, item count == page count, all descriptions non-empty; cmp the two feed.xml files byte-identical.

check.sh at plugin-subsystem root running three end-to-end scenarios against a tmp fixture site via forge.py wired to plugin_loader: (1) both plugins enabled -> output HTML contains tok- spans AND feed.xml exists and parses; (2) "plugins": [] -> zero tok- spans in any output file, no feed.xml; (3) fixture plugin that raises -> build exits nonzero with plugin name in stderr. Non-interactive, own tmp dirs, trap cleanup, <60s, safe under make -j2.
